items.title: slop-guard docs
items.text:
slop-guard is a rule-based prose linter for formulaic AI writing. It scores text from 0 to 100, points to the exact spans that pulled the score down, and returns direct advice for the rewrite.

This site is versioned by release. Use the selector in the header to move between tagged releases and dev (main) when you want the current state of the default branch.

items.title: Hello world
items.text:
Run the CLI once with uvx:

uvx --from slop-guard sg README.md

That command installs the published package into a temporary environment, lints the file, and prints a compact score line. When you need the exact matches and rewrite guidance, rerun it with -v:

uvx --from slop-guard sg -v README.md

Continue with Get Started for installation and local workflows, or jump to Agents for Codex and Claude Code setup.

items.title: Agents
items.text:
Both supported clients use the same MCP command:

uvx slop-guard

That starts the local MCP server without any API calls or model-side judging. The tools return structured diagnostics that agents can use while they draft docs, release notes, or status updates.

The MCP surface is small on purpose. Agents can call check_slop when they already have text in memory, or check_slop_file when they need to inspect a file on disk. Both tools return structured JSON, so the client can point at exact spans and feed the advice back into the rewrite loop.

items.title: Codex
items.text:
Register the server with Codex by pointing mcp add at uvx slop-guard, or add it to ~/.codex/config.toml:

[mcp_servers.slop-guard]
command = "uvx"
args = ["slop-guard"]

items.title: Claude Code
items.text:
Register the server with Claude Code by pointing mcp add at uvx slop-guard, or add it to .mcp.json:

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "slop-guard": {
      "command": "uvx",
      "args": ["slop-guard"]
    }
  }
}

items.title: Pin a release
items.text:
If an automation or team workflow needs a fixed package version, pin it in the command arguments:

uvx slop-guard==0.4.1

Use the release selector in this documentation site when you want the matching docs for that pinned version.

Every published docs page also exposes a raw Markdown sibling at the same slug. For example, the rendered get-started page lives alongside /docs/get-started.md, which makes scripted crawling easier for agents and automation.

If you are deciding between a release and dev (main), use a release for stable automation and use dev (main) when you are testing current repository behavior before the next tag ships.

items.title: Get started
items.text: slop-guard works as a command-line linter and as an MCP server for coding agents. The fastest way to try it is to run the CLI once with uvx.

items.title: Run once
items.text:
uvx --from slop-guard sg README.md

That command downloads the current release, runs the linter, and exits without installing a long-lived tool.

items.title: Install the CLI
items.text:
If you want a persistent command, install the package as a uv tool:

uv tool install slop-guard
sg README.md

You can pin a specific release when reproducibility matters:

uv tool install slop-guard==0.4.1

items.title: Gate prose in CI
items.text:
Use a threshold when you want prose checks to fail the build:

sg -t 60 README.md docs/**/*.md

Add -v to inspect individual hits, or -j when you want JSON output for scripts.

items.title: Work from source
items.text:
From a local checkout, uv run exposes the same entry points without publishing a package first:

uv run sg README.md
uv run slop-guard
uv run sg-fit --help

If you are working on this repository's documentation site, these project targets cover the Zensical workflow:

make docs-serve
make docs-build
make docs-check

items.title: Rule library
items.text:
slop-guard ships a pipeline of small, independently scored rules. Each rule targets one formulaic pattern, flags the exact matching spans, and contributes a penalty toward the final score.

The default pipeline runs 24 rules across four scopes. Open a card to read the full rule page, including example violations, default thresholds, and a link to the source file.

items.title: Word rules
items.text:
Single-token checks. These are the cheapest rules and fire on individual AI-associated words.

Slop Word

Detect overused AI-associated slop words.

items.title: Sentence rules
items.text:
One sentence at a time. Catches canned phrases, disclosures, tone markers, and templated pivots.

Slop Phrase

Detect stock slop phrases and transition templates.

Tone Marker

Detect AI-style tone markers and opener tells.

Weasel Phrase

Detect unattributed weasel phrases.

AI Disclosure

Detect direct AI self-disclosure statements.

Placeholder

Detect unfinished placeholder markers.

Contrast Pair

Detect repeated contrast constructions that stage a binary opposition.

Intrasentence Keyword Bold

Detect short, mid-sentence keyword bold spans (LLM emphasis tic).

Setup Resolution

Detect setup-resolution rhetorical flips.

Pithy Fragment

Detect short evaluative pivot fragments.

items.title: Paragraph rules
items.text:
Adjacent-line structure. Catches bullet runs, blockquotes, horizontal rules, and listicle layouts.

Structural Pattern

Detect listicle-like structural patterns in paragraphs.

Bullet Density

Detect bullet-heavy document formatting.

Blockquote Density

Detect excessive thesis-style blockquote usage.

Bold Term Bullet Run

Detect runs of bullets that start with bold terms.

Horizontal Rule Overuse

Detect overuse of horizontal rule separators.

items.title: Passage rules
items.text:
Whole-document signals. Catches rhythm, density, and repetition across the full text.

Rhythm

Detect monotonous sentence-length rhythm.

Em Dash Density

Detect overuse of em dashes across a passage.

Colon Density

Detect elaboration-colon overuse in prose passages.

Phrase Reuse

Detect repeated long n-gram phrase reuse.

Copula Chain

Detect high copula-sentence density - an encyclopedic AI chain pattern.

Extreme Sentence

Detect extremely long run-on sentences.

Closing Aphorism

Detect moralizing or generalizing closing sentences.

Paragraph Balance

Detect suspiciously uniform paragraph lengths.

Paragraph CV

Detect suspiciously uniform paragraph lengths.

items.title: AI Disclosure
items.text:
SENTENCE AI Disclosure source

Detect direct AI self-disclosure statements.

Class AIDisclosureRule Rule name ai_disclosure Count key ai_disclosure

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"As an AI language model, I cannot browse the web."Explicitly discloses model identity and capability limits. Flag"I am just an AI, so I do not have personal experience."First-person model disclaimer breaks authorial voice. Pass"The report uses only the provided dataset."States scope directly without AI identity disclosure. Pass"I do not have evidence for that claim."Epistemic limitation without model-specific boilerplate.

items.title: Severity
items.text: High; disclosure phrases are strong and explicit AI-origin signals.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: context_window_chars60 penalty-10

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Blockquote Density
items.text:
PARAGRAPH Blockquote Density source

Detect excessive thesis-style blockquote usage.

Class BlockquoteDensityRule Rule name structural Count key blockquote_density

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagMultiple consecutive lines starting with ">" for key arguments.Heavy quote styling replaces integrated prose argumentation. FlagRepeated pull-quote sections throughout a short document.Presentation style becomes formulaic. PassOne short quotation used to cite a source.Quote usage is limited and justified. PassCode blocks and normal paragraphs without blockquote overuse.Structural emphasis remains balanced.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium; usually indicates a style issue rather than factual error.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: cap4 free_lines2 min_lines3 penalty_step-3

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Bold Term Bullet Run
items.text:
PARAGRAPH Bold Term Bullet Run source

Detect runs of bullets that start with bold terms.

Class BoldTermBulletRunRule Rule name structural Count key bold_bullet_list

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"- Reliability ...\n- Scalability ...\n- Security ..."Consecutive bold-term bullets create rigid templated structure. FlagNumbered items where each starts with a bold label.Repetitive lead-term pattern dominates the section. PassA short list with plain bullet text and no bold lead labels.List exists without the specific template shape. PassParagraph text with occasional inline bold for emphasis.Bold usage is not tied to repetitive bullet starts.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium to high when long runs appear in the same section.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: min_run_length3 penalty-5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Bullet Density
items.text:
PARAGRAPH Bullet Density source

Detect bullet-heavy document formatting.

Class BulletDensityRule Rule name structural Count key bullet_density

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagA section where most lines begin with "-", "*", or numbered bullets.High bullet ratio indicates list dominance. FlagA long checklist with minimal paragraph text.Formatting overwhelms narrative flow. PassOne short bullet list embedded in otherwise normal prose.Bullets are used sparingly for clarity. PassParagraph-only explanatory text.No list dominance.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium to high depending on how much of the passage is list-form.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: penalty-8 ratio_threshold0.4

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Closing Aphorism
items.text:
PASSAGE Closing Aphorism source

Detect moralizing or generalizing closing sentences.

Class ClosingAphorismRule Rule name closing_aphorism Count key closing_aphorism

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"Ultimately, it is our choices that define the system we build."Abstract generalization that wraps up abstractly with "ultimately". Flag"That's the real challenge in any distributed system.""That's the X" closing flourish. Pass"The build finished in 4.2 seconds."Specific and concrete - no moral framing. Pass"We shipped it on Friday."Concrete action, not an abstraction.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low; fires at most once per passage on the closing sentence only.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: min_sentences4 penalty-4

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @SinatrasC @eric-tramel

items.title: Colon Density
items.text:
PASSAGE Colon Density source

Detect elaboration-colon overuse in prose passages.

Class ColonDensityRule Rule name colon_density Count key colon_density

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"Key point: we should retry: then back off: then log."Multiple prose colons create a formulaic explanatory cadence. FlagRepeated mid-sentence "X: y..." patterns throughout one passage.Elaboration punctuation becomes excessive. Pass"https://example.com:443" and JSON snippets in code fences.Technical colons are excluded from scoring logic. PassOccasional colon in a heading-like sentence.Limited use remains within normal prose style.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low to medium; punctuation style signal that compounds with others.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: density_threshold1.5 penalty-3 words_basis150.0

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Contrast Pair
items.text:
SENTENCE Contrast Pair source

Detect repeated contrast constructions that stage a binary opposition.

Class ContrastPairRule Rule name contrast_pair Count key contrast_pairs

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"This is focus, not frenzy."Uses the targeted "A, not B" shape. Flag"The platform is not only fast but also reliable."Uses the staged "not only X but Y" frame. Pass"This approach prioritizes focus over speed."Contrast exists, but not in the repetitive pattern form. Pass"The design reduces complexity while improving clarity."Balanced comparison without slogan-like structure.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low per instance, medium when repeated frequently in one passage.

items.title: Notes
items.text: Detection is purely pattern-based — legitimate contrasts may still be flagged when density exceeds the configured threshold. Adjust advice_min to tune when summary advice appears.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: advice_min2 context_window_chars60 penalty-1 record_cap5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Copula Chain
items.text:
PASSAGE Copula Chain source

Detect high copula-sentence density - an encyclopedic AI chain pattern.

Class CopulaChainRule Rule name copula_chain Count key copula_chain

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"Python is a language. Lists are mutable. Dicts are mappings. Sets areunordered. Tuples are immutable. Generators are lazy." Over half the sentences open with a copula verb. Pass"Python handles this with generators. You can use a list comprehension.This avoids materialising the whole sequence." Copula density stays well below the threshold.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium; a single passage-level flag with concentrated penalty.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: min_sentences6 penalty-3 threshold0.5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @SinatrasC @eric-tramel

items.title: Em Dash Density
items.text:
PASSAGE Em Dash Density source

Detect overuse of em dashes across a passage.

Class EmDashDensityRule Rule name em_dash Count key em_dash

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"The plan works -- quickly -- and scales -- in production."Multiple dash interruptions in a short span. FlagFrequent " -- " or unicode em dash usage above configured density.Dash rate exceeds expected prose baseline. PassOccasional em dash used once for emphasis in a long section.Stylistic punctuation remains moderate. PassPunctuation primarily uses commas and periods with clear sentence flow.No overreliance on dash cadence.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low to medium; stylistic alone, but meaningful when persistent.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: density_threshold1.0 penalty-3 words_basis150.0

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Extreme Sentence
items.text:
PASSAGE Extreme Sentence source

Detect extremely long run-on sentences.

Class ExtremeSentenceRule Rule name extreme_sentence Count key extreme_sentence

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagA single sentence of 90 words chaining subordinate clause after subordinateclause with commas and conjunctions. The sheer length signals generated rather than drafted prose. Pass"The update shipped on Tuesday." - short, direct sentence. PassA passage where every sentence stays under 40 words.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium; each hit adds structural evidence of generated text.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: min_words140 penalty-5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @SinatrasC @eric-tramel

items.title: Horizontal Rule Overuse
items.text:
PARAGRAPH Horizontal Rule Overuse source

Detect overuse of horizontal rule separators.

Class HorizontalRuleOveruseRule Rule name structural Count key horizontal_rules

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagRepeated "---" lines between many short sections.Divider count exceeds reasonable editorial use. FlagAlternating headers and horizontal rules throughout a brief note.Layout feels scaffolded rather than authored. PassOne divider between two major sections.Limited structural use is acceptable. PassSectioning done with headings and paragraph transitions only.No excessive visual separators.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low to medium; mostly a formatting signal unless heavily repeated.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: min_count4 penalty-3

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Intrasentence Keyword Bold
items.text:
SENTENCE Intrasentence Keyword Bold source

Detect short, mid-sentence keyword bold spans (LLM emphasis tic).

Class IntrasentenceKeywordBoldRule Rule name intrasentence_keyword_bold Count key intrasentence_keyword_bold

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"We need to carefully consider all the options before deciding."Mid-sentence bold span used as arbitrary keyword emphasis. Flag"The system must remain highly available during peak hours."Two-word emphasis inserted into otherwise normal prose. Pass"Note: make sure to back up your data first."Bold acts as a labeled lead-in at the start of the line. Pass"## A section heading without inline emphasis"Markdown heading line with no inline bold. Pass"The release shipped on schedule and the team celebrated."Plain prose without any bold formatting.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low per instance, medium when repeated frequently in one passage.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: advice_min3 context_window_chars60 max_words5 penalty-2 record_cap5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @chris-alexiuk

items.title: Paragraph Balance
items.text:
PASSAGE Paragraph Balance source

Detect suspiciously uniform paragraph lengths.

Class ParagraphBalanceRule Rule name paragraph_balance Count key paragraph_balance

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagA multi-paragraph essay where every paragraph is 40-50 words.Both rules would flag the near-identical lengths. PassA passage with one two-sentence paragraph and one eight-sentence sprawl.The high length variance passes both rules cleanly.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low to medium; stronger when combined with other rhythm signals.

items.title: Two complementary rules are provided
items.text: ParagraphBalanceRule - fires when the shortest body paragraph is more than a configured fraction of the longest (min/max ratio too high). ParagraphCVRule - fires when the coefficient of variation of all paragraph word-counts falls below a threshold (low spread = formulaic rhythm).

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: balance_threshold0.7 min_body_paragraphs3 penalty-6

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @SinatrasC @eric-tramel

items.title: Paragraph CV
items.text:
PASSAGE Paragraph CV source

Detect suspiciously uniform paragraph lengths.

Class ParagraphCVRule Rule name paragraph_cv Count key paragraph_cv

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagA multi-paragraph essay where every paragraph is 40-50 words.Both rules would flag the near-identical lengths. PassA passage with one two-sentence paragraph and one eight-sentence sprawl.The high length variance passes both rules cleanly.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low to medium; stronger when combined with other rhythm signals.

items.title: Two complementary rules are provided
items.text: ParagraphBalanceRule - fires when the shortest body paragraph is more than a configured fraction of the longest (min/max ratio too high). ParagraphCVRule - fires when the coefficient of variation of all paragraph word-counts falls below a threshold (low spread = formulaic rhythm).

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: cv_threshold0.45 min_paragraphs5 penalty-1

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @SinatrasC @eric-tramel

items.title: Phrase Reuse
items.text:
PASSAGE Phrase Reuse source

Detect repeated long n-gram phrase reuse.

Class PhraseReuseRule Rule name phrase_reuse Count key phrase_reuse

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagRepeating "at the end of the day" many times in one document.Same phrase recurs instead of varied expression. FlagReusing a 4-8 word clause across multiple paragraphs.Long repeated n-grams suggest copy-pattern generation. PassRepeating short stopword-heavy fragments like "in the end".Common function phrases are filtered or suppressed. PassUsing related ideas with different wording across sections.Semantic repetition without lexical cloning is acceptable.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium to high; repeated long phrases are strong formulaicity signals.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: penalty-1 record_cap5 repeated_ngram_max_n8 repeated_ngram_min_count3 repeated_ngram_min_n4

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Pithy Fragment
items.text:
SENTENCE Pithy Fragment source

Detect short evaluative pivot fragments.

Class PithyFragmentRule Rule name pithy_fragment Count key pithy_fragment

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"Simple, but powerful."Short evaluative fragment with pivot conjunction. Flag"Fast, yet reliable."Compact slogan-like pivot pattern. Pass"The service is simple to run but expensive at peak load."Full sentence with concrete tradeoff detail. Pass"It is fast and reliable in this benchmark."Plain claim without fragment-style punch line.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low to medium; mostly stylistic alone, stronger when clustered.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: max_sentence_words6 penalty-2 record_cap3

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Placeholder
items.text:
SENTENCE Placeholder source

Detect unfinished placeholder markers.

Class PlaceholderRule Rule name placeholder Count key placeholder

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"[insert source citation]"Placeholder was left unresolved in final prose. Flag"Contact: [your email here]"Template token is present instead of real content. Pass"Contact: security@example.com"Real value is present and complete. Pass"The appendix lists all citations."No unresolved placeholder syntax.

items.title: Severity
items.text: High for publication quality; placeholders are explicit unfinished content and should generally be fixed before release.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: context_window_chars60 penalty-5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Rhythm
items.text:
PASSAGE Rhythm source

Detect monotonous sentence-length rhythm.

Class RhythmRule Rule name rhythm Count key rhythm

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy FlagA long paragraph where nearly every sentence has similar token length.Low variation creates flat, synthetic rhythm. FlagFive to ten sentences all around the same size and pacing.Statistical variance falls below the configured threshold. PassMixed sentence lengths with short emphatic lines and longer explanation.Natural rhythm diversity is present. PassA concise note with too few sentences for robust rhythm inference.Rule does not apply when sample size is small.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium; a useful style signal that is stronger with other findings.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: cv_threshold0.3 min_sentences5 penalty-5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Setup Resolution
items.text:
SENTENCE Setup Resolution source

Detect setup-resolution rhetorical flips.

Class SetupResolutionRule Rule name setup_resolution Count key setup_resolution

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"This is not about tooling. It is about discipline."Classic setup then immediate reframing resolution. Flag"It is not random; it is deliberate."Same flip pattern with compressed punctuation. Pass"The problem is tooling and team discipline."States both ideas directly without staged reversal. Pass"This approach emphasizes discipline over tooling."Comparative statement without setup-resolution cadence.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium; repeated occurrences strongly suggest formulaic generation.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: context_window_chars60 penalty-3 record_cap5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Slop Phrase
items.text:
SENTENCE Slop Phrase source

Detect stock slop phrases and transition templates.

Class SlopPhraseRule Rule name slop_phrase Count key slop_phrases

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"It's worth noting that reliability matters."Uses a canned setup phrase instead of stating the claim directly. Flag"If you want, I can adapt this into a checklist."Uses assistant-menu phrasing that breaks authored prose voice. Pass"Reliability matters because retries hide partial failures."Direct assertion with rationale and no framing template. Pass"The next section covers deployment constraints."Legitimate transition written in plain style.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium; each hit is a strong indicator of templated writing style.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: context_window_chars60 penalty-3

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel @SinatrasC

items.title: Slop Word
items.text:
WORD Slop Word source

Detect overused AI-associated slop words.

Class SlopWordRule Rule name slop_word Count key slop_words

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"This is a crucial, groundbreaking paradigm for modern teams."Uses stacked hype words instead of concrete claims. Flag"We can seamlessly leverage a robust framework to unlock outcomes."Uses multiple stock verbs and adjectives common in template prose. Pass"This patch removes an O(n^2) loop in the tokenizer."Specific technical claim with no hype vocabulary. Pass"P95 latency dropped from 180 ms to 95 ms after batching writes."Concrete measurement, not promotional phrasing.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Low to medium per hit; repeated hits are stronger evidence of generic language and accumulate penalty quickly.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: context_window_chars60 penalty-2

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel @SinatrasC

items.title: Structural Pattern
items.text:
PARAGRAPH Structural Pattern source

Detect listicle-like structural patterns in paragraphs.

Class StructuralPatternRule Rule name structural Count key structural

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"Problem: ... Solution: ... Result: ..."Repeated bold-header blocks produce rigid listicle framing. Flag"Reliable, scalable, and maintainable."Triadic pattern used repeatedly creates synthetic cadence. Pass"The section opens with one heading followed by normal paragraphs."Uses structure, but not repetitive patterning. Pass"The system is reliable and maintainable at this workload."Natural phrasing without triadic slogan cadence.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium to high when multiple structural signals co-occur.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: bold_header_min3 bold_header_penalty-5 bullet_run_min6 bullet_run_penalty-3 context_window_chars60 triadic_advice_min3 triadic_penalty-1 triadic_record_cap5

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Tone Marker
items.text:
SENTENCE Tone Marker source

Detect AI-style tone markers and opener tells.

Class ToneMarkerRule Rule name tone Count key tone

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"Would you like me to provide a shorter version?"Meta-conversation phrasing is an AI tell in authored prose. Flag"Certainly, this approach works in most environments."Formulaic opener pattern used by assistant responses. Pass"This approach works in most environments."Same claim without assistant-style framing. Pass"The failure occurred after the second retry."Neutral narration without scripted dramatic setup.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium to high; often a strong signal of assistant-authored tone.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: context_window_chars60 sentence_opener_penalty-2 tone_penalty-3

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel

items.title: Weasel Phrase
items.text:
SENTENCE Weasel Phrase source

Detect unattributed weasel phrases.

Class WeaselPhraseRule Rule name weasel Count key weasel

items.title: Behavior
items.text: ResultInput textWhy Flag"Many believe this framework is the future."Uses anonymous consensus instead of evidence. Flag"Studies show the feature improves trust."Claims research support without any citation context. Pass"A 2024 ACM paper reports a 12 percent error reduction."Provides concrete source context. Pass"We expect lower error rates based on last quarter's logs."Owns the claim and basis directly.

items.title: Severity
items.text: Medium; each hit indicates weak attribution and rhetorical padding.

items.title: Default configuration
items.text: context_window_chars60 penalty-2

items.title: Contributors
items.text: @eric-tramel
